items.title: Man who fatally shot officer Keith Smith sentenced to 40 years in jail

children.content: Tasmanian man Leigh Sushames shot dead Keith Smith last year when the police constable tried to serve him with a home repossession notice.

items.title: Former cricketer pleads guilty to underage sexual abuse charges

children.content: Aaron William Summers was playing for the Hobart Hurricanes in January 2018 when the underage sexual abuse occurred, a Tasmanian court has heard.

image.altText: A man in a white hoodie and baseball cap walking

items.title: Man suing Spirit of Tasmania operator told he 'should have sat' on ship's toilet

children.content: A man who claims to have sustained injuries after falling over while using a toilet at sea says the ferry operator was negligent, the federal court has heard.

image.altText: A large white and yellow cruise ship sits in the waterfront of a city.

items.title: Menopause and perimenopause support expands into nationwide clinics

children.content: Access to physiotherapy, dietetics and psychology services will become routine in a national expansion to menopause and perimenopause support from July 1.

items.title: Hobart stadium developer not supportive of waterfront precinct proposal

children.content: A proposal to build an underground car park, apartments and retail spaces neighbouring the Macquarie Point stadium site "does not appear to align with the precinct plan", stadium developer says.

image.altText: Renders of proposed housing, retail spaces and car parking at Hobart's Regatta Point by Regatta Quay Precinct.

items.title: Neil the seal's unique lifestyle may help protect him from bird flu

children.content: Experts say it is a matter of when, not if, a deadly strain of bird flu reaches Tasmania, and popular marine visitor Neil the seal is not isolated from the threat, despite his relatively solo lifestyle.

image.altText: Neil the Seal, a juvenile elephant seal seen by a roadside in a suburban seaside neighbourhood.

items.title: TassieCast: What's behind Tasmania's drop in vaccination rates?

children.content: Tasmania is experiencing a whooping cough epidemic. It's one of the highly contagious diseases that can be prevented through vaccination, but like nationally, fewer Tasmanians are keeping up to date with their jabs. Airlie Ward chats with reporter Selina Bryan.

items.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation after ship reports attack

children.content: The UN pauses its operation to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel reports an attack.

image.altText: A ship in the forground and another moving a long the horizon the background.

items.title: Another suspected H5 bird flu case detected in WA

children.content: Another suspected positive case of the H5 bird flu has been detected in Western Australia, marking the fifth confirmed or suspected case in Australia.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder-length hair is speaking to media.

items.title: How China's new 'ethnic unity' law could target people in Australia

children.content: The Australian government raises concerns directly with China over the country's new ethnic unity law, which allows Beijing to target people overseas.

items.title: 'ISIS bride' charged with slavery offences is granted bail

children.content: A court grants bail for Melbourne grandmother Kawsar Ahmad, who is alleged to have owned a slave while in Syria under Islamic State rule.

image.altText: A woman wearing a brown hijab sits in court.

items.title: More than 400 fraud charges laid against former strata manager

children.content: Jessica Marrie Carah is alleged to have transferred more than $2 million out of strata trust accounts.

image.altText: A woman holds a bouquet of flowers.

items.title: No-show cuttlefish result in million-dollar loss for regional SA town

children.content: Whyalla has been forced to wind back services and let staff let go as its tourism industry suffers million-dollar losses from cuttlefish not making their usual migration.

items.title: Position switch the latest chapter in Bulldogs' experiment against Titans

children.content: Canterbury's Stephen Crichton starts at five-eighth on the Gold Coast after making a mid-game switch with Matt Burton last week. Follow live.

items.title: Taxi company fined following deaths of two passengers in wheelchairs

children.content: A Victorian taxi company has been fined over the separate 2021 deaths of two passengers who used wheelchairs.

items.title: How I save money by storing and cooking things differently

children.content: I've developed simple storage and cooking systems that keep food visible and fresh while ensuring I use things up. Here's how I make cooking for one feel more doable, affordable and lower-impact.

items.title: Why the Moana live action movie has come so soon

items.title: Supergirl shoots for the moon, but runs out of fuel

children.content: Australian Milly Alcock shines as Supergirl in the hero's first solo outing since 1984. If only the rest of the movie sparkled around her.

items.title: Comparing Trump's Iran negotiations to what Obama already did

children.content: The Trump administration has been negotiating directly with Iran this week in an effort to reach a long-term peace agreement, but it has several hurdles to clear before it can better the deal the United States already had.

items.title: Judge rejects role of Ozempic in grandmother's roadside stabbing attack

children.content: A 60-year-old woman who stabbed her grandson and daughter-in-law has been sentenced to jail. Her defence team had argued she was suffering sudden and extreme anger partially induced by weight-loss medication.

image.altText: A group of police in uniform stand beside a four wheel drive car on the side of the road.

items.title: H5N1 emerged in Asia 30 years ago. Here's what happened next

children.content: Experts say Asia's decades-long experience fighting bird flu has shown there is no magic bullet and that fragmented responses to the disease will fail.

items.title: NT Labor MLA pledges change after another speeding fine revelation

items.title: Youth offender program provider accused of misappropriating public funds

items.title: Warning fire ants could kill thousands of pets a year

children.content: If Australia does not ramp up fire ant eradication efforts, the invasive species will cause billion-dollar economic impacts and dozens of deaths, a study finds.

items.title: Queensland to introduce new drink and drug driving laws to fix 'loophole'

children.content: The Crisafulli government has announced a crackdown on dangerous driving while doubling down on a "zero-tolerance" approach to drug driving, including for medical cannabis users.

items.title: SA Police officers 'did not act appropriately' in handling of DV survivor's case

items.title: Matthew Guy says he's owed apology over Moira Deeming assault allegation

items.title: Mixed reaction to beach town at forefront of over-50s lifestyle resort boom
